name: Can I sue Perplexity if they used my blog post in their answers?
description: Yes, if they copied substantial original content; register copyright and gather proof of use.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue Perplexity if they used my blog post in their answers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if they copied substantial original content; register copyright and gather proof of use.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, if Perplexity reproduced substantial portions of your copyrighted blog without permission, you may have a viable copyright claim similar to publishers' cases.[1][2] Context: Cases like NYT v. Perplexity allege direct infringement via scraping and output generation.[2][3] Key factors: Prove ownership/registration, direct copying (even paywalled), market harm; fair use defense likely argued but failing so far.[1][4] Register your work first via copyright.gov for statutory damages. Track queries showing your content; file in federal court where Perplexity operates, like New York. Seek IP attorney evaluation.
